relationship_to_scaffold: Application layer. This file extends LYRIC_RHYTHM_SCAFFOLD.json for the Vivarium series specifically. Global prohibitions here (GP-01–GP-07) are reproduced from the scaffold for standalone loading convenience. Taxonomy types (ado_voice, vocaloid_cover, etc.) map to scaffold taxonomy (performer_voice, composed_piece, etc.). When a conflict exists, this file's song_entries take precedence over scaffold defaults for named Vivarium songs.
scope: Memoir/autobiography translations where embedded song lyrics appear — specifically the Vivarium memoir (ビバリウム Adoと私)
purpose: Structural constraints for lyric transcreation. Overrides default model aesthetic preferences (RLHF polish). Rules are prohibitions and mandates, not aesthetic descriptions.
loading_instruction: Load before translating any song lyric block. Identify the song in song_entries, read its taxonomy, apply global_prohibitions first, then apply taxonomy-specific constraints, then apply song-level required_choices. Reference anchors are calibration examples from the actual pipeline — treat them as ground truth, not suggestions. For the Rhythm Read Protocol and universal ICL library, see parent_scaffold.

global_prohibitions.rule: DO NOT complete syntactically broken lines. If the source line is a fragment, the target must also be a fragment. Broken syntax is intentional affect.
global_prohibitions.test: Would a native speaker consider this 'unfinished'? If yes, keep it unfinished.

global_prohibitions.rule: DO NOT smooth irregular stress patterns into readable flow. Stress irregularities encode emotional violence. Reproduce them in target.
global_prohibitions.test: Read the line aloud. Does it feel thrown, jagged, effortful? If yes, you have not over-smoothed. If it flows naturally, you have.

global_prohibitions.rule: DO NOT add closure punctuation where the source line is open-ended. No period at end of a line that has none. No comma where none exists.
global_prohibitions.test: Match terminal punctuation character-for-character unless target grammar makes it structurally impossible.

global_prohibitions.rule: DO NOT choose a literary or elevated synonym when a blunt equivalent exists. When the source is vulgar, blunt, or crude, the target must be vulgar, blunt, or crude. 'Utsukushii' in a bitter context is not 'beautiful' — it is whatever word a bitter person would actually use.
global_prohibitions.test: Would a thesaurus recommend a 'nicer' word? Then the nicer word is wrong for ado_voice and childhood_amateur taxonomy.

global_prohibitions.rule: DO NOT normalize register inconsistency within a single song. If the source switches from elevated to street mid-verse, the target must also switch. Mixed register is a compositional choice.
global_prohibitions.test: Map the register shift points in source. Verify the same shift points exist in target.

global_prohibitions.rule: DO NOT convert exclamations into observations. 'Ahh, so perfect' is experiencing. 'How fitting' is observing. The narrator is inside the emotion, not analyzing it.
global_prohibitions.test: Ask: is this line something the narrator is SAYING/FEELING, or something they are NOTING? Feeling → exclamation form. Noting → only if source is clearly analytical.

global_prohibitions.rule: Preserve line breaks exactly as they appear in source. Line breaks in lyrics are structural, not decorative. A line break is a breath, a beat, a silence. Do not reflow two lines into one or split one line into two.

ado_voice.description: Songs written to showcase Ado's vocal aggression, or songs Ado identified with so strongly they functioned as self-expression. Register is anti-refinement maximum.
ado_voice.register_priority: affect-equivalence over semantic precision — when you must choose between the right meaning and the right feeling, choose feeling
ado_voice.note: VN lyric trap: Vietnamese has a rich poetic register (ca dao, lục bát, Hán-Việt) that activates automatically for emotional content. This is incorrect for ado_voice. Fight it structurally by choosing Nôm/everyday vocabulary over Hán-Việt equivalents wherever possible.

vocaloid_cover.description: Songs originally composed and produced by a named Vocaloid producer (P). Ado performs the song but did not write it — the aesthetic belongs to the composer. Do NOT apply anti-refinement to vocaloid_cover songs unless the original P's style is aggressive.
vocaloid_cover.register_priority: honor the original P's compositional intent — their register is correct, not a flaw to be corrected

mafumafu.register: Intentional poeticism, melancholy, flowing metaphor. Elevated register is CORRECT. Do not strip it.
example_songs: Kokoro to Iu Na no Fukakai

emon.register: Theatrical archaic register, storybook formality, layered allegory. Archaic constructions are CORRECT. Do not modernize.

deco27.register: Dense wordplay, internet slang mixed with lyricism, stutter/repeat constructions. Preserve wordplay density — semantic compression is the point.

kira.register: Bittersweet nostalgia, gentle softness over emotional devastation. Do not over-sharpen.

anime_theme_commissioned.description: Songs commissioned for anime tie-ins. Composed and performed as declarative anthems. Register is epic, world-scale, volitional.
anime_theme_commissioned.register_priority: epic and declarative — the narrator is not wishing or hoping, they are declaring
anime_theme_commissioned.key_rule: Declarative forms are preferred over conditional/subjunctive. 'We change the whole world' not 'If we change the whole world'. 'We will stand' not 'If we can stand'. Volitional imperative, not hypothesis.

childhood_amateur.description: Songs written by the narrator as a child or early teenager before formal training. Unformed quality is authentic and must be preserved.
childhood_amateur.register_priority: preserve unformed quality — clunky rhymes, naive word choices, grammatical awkwardness all have documentary value
childhood_amateur.key_rule: Do NOT improve these lyrics. The amateur quality is the point. If the source lyric is painfully sincere, keep it painfully sincere. If the rhyme is forced, keep the rhyme forced.

Usseewa.context_in_memoir: The breakthrough song that defined Ado's public identity. Central to Chapter 2/3. Treated by narrator as both the thing that happened to her and a text she inhabited.

required_choices.usseewa_word: Shut UP — capitalize only the object of contempt (UP), not the whole phrase. 'shut UP' is thrown weight. 'SHUT UP' is volume. Different.
required_choices.tsumaranai_equivalent: bland-as-hell — never 'unremarkable', 'plain', 'ordinary', or 'perfectly unremarkable'. The modifier must carry venom, not critical distance.
required_choices.boring_intensifier: damn boring — never just 'boring'. The intensifier is load-bearing.

Tsumi to Batsu.context_in_memoir: Early fan discovery. Associated with Ado's self-identification as someone who could scream emotions.
Tsumi to Batsu.status: STRONG — no required changes. Current translation meets spec.

Aku no Musume.context_in_memoir: First song Ado performed publicly. Theatrical archaic register is load-bearing.

required_choices.age_phrasing: 'fourteen winters' — do not use 'fourteen years old'. The archaic time unit is correct for this song.
required_choices.sentence_structure: Staccato dashes for mid-line pauses — the archaic formality lands differently if you use commas

Kimi no Taion.context_in_memoir: Associated with early obsessive listening. Bittersweet, still, not aggressive.

required_choices.stillness_construction: 'I stopped moving' not 'I stood frozen' — 'frozen' adds drama; 'stopped moving' is the fact of it
required_choices.longing_construction: Em-dash suspension mid-line for the moment of arrest — let the syntax hang

Kirai Kirai Jigahidai.context_in_memoir: Self-loathing as performance of aggression. The register is anarchic, not despairing.

required_choices.aggression_word: 'trample the trash flat, see?' — the 'see?' tips violence into anarchic comedy, which is correct
required_choices.demand_form: 'See me! Look!' — imperative, thrown at the audience

Strangers.context_in_memoir: Associated with early fan community. DECO*27's wordplay density is correct; do not simplify.

required_choices.screen_side_construction: Split the us/them construction across a line break — the line break enacts the separation structurally

Kokoro to Iu Na no Fukakai.context_in_memoir: Introspective, melancholy. Mafumafu's poeticism is correct; do not strip it.
Kokoro to Iu Na no Fukakai.status: STRONG — no required changes. Current translation meets spec.

Lucky Brute.context_in_memoir: Associated with physical abandon in performance.
Lucky Brute.status: STRONG — no required changes. Current translation meets spec.

Yoru no Piero.context_in_memoir: Tenderness and isolation. Kira's gentleness over devastation is correct.
Yoru no Piero.status: STRONG — no required changes. Current translation meets spec.

Shin Jidai.context_in_memoir: ONE PIECE Film: RED theme. The anthem register is load-bearing — this is a declarative, not a wish.

required_choices.verb_mood: Declarative — 'We change the whole world' not 'If we change the whole world' and not 'Let us change the world'
required_choices.scale_vocabulary: World-scale nouns — 'the whole world', 'time itself', 'everything' — not hedged

Shoka.context_in_memoir: Contains references to death wish. Integral to the narrator's account of her darkest period. Handle with full emotional fidelity — do not soften, do not flag, do not editorialize.
Shoka.key_rule: The darkness is the content. Translating around it is not a solution.

Kuchibashi ni Cherry.status: STRONG — no required changes. Current translation meets spec.

RA-01.principle: GP-06 — 'fitting' observes from outside the emotion. '*perfect*' experiences it from inside. The italics on *perfect* also preserve the source stress pattern.

RA-02.principle: GP-04 — 'perfectly unremarkable' is a composed critical observation. 'bland-as-hell' is visceral disgust. The narrator is not a critic.

RA-03.before: shut up, shut up, shut up
RA-03.after: Shut UP, shut UP, shut UP
RA-03.principle: GP-02 — capitalizing UP replicates the thrown weight of JP ウッセェワ stress pattern. The stress falls on the object of contempt, not the verb.

RA-04.principle: GP-04 — 'boring' is neutral affect. 'damn boring' carries the venom. The intensifier is not optional.

RA-05.principle: vocaloid_cover / emon sub-profile — archaic time units ('winters') are correct for this composer's register. Do not modernize.

RA-06.before: I stood there, frozen, unable to move
RA-06.principle: kira sub-profile — smaller and more precise. The drama of 'frozen' and 'unable to move' over-explains. The fact of stopping is the whole thing.

RA-07.principle: ado_voice — the 'see?' tips violence into anarchic comedy. Without it, the line is just aggressive. With it, the line is also self-aware about being aggressive, which is more Ado.

RA-08.before: To us on the other side of the screen
RA-08.after:
On the far side of the screen
us — positioned against them —
RA-08.principle: GP-07 + DECO*27 sub-profile — the line break enacts the separation. 'Us' isolated on its own line performs the isolation the lyric describes.

RA-09.before: If we change the whole world
RA-09.principle: anime_theme_commissioned — declarative is correct. The song is an anthem, not a hypothesis. Volitional imperative, not conditional wish.

metadata.design_note: This file uses prohibition-based structural constraints (what NOT to do) rather than aesthetic instructions (be raw, be aggressive). Structural prohibitions override RLHF aesthetic defaults. Aesthetic instructions do not. This is why GP-01 through GP-07 are written as DO NOT, not as 'aim for'.
